# SundAI

> SundAI is a 100% European AI governance, AI security, responsible adoption, automation and training studio operated by RIIMON HOLDINGS LTD (company no. 13813344).

## Primary description
SundAI helps European small and mid-sized organisations, public and social services, education providers, NGOs, regulated organisations and knowledge-intensive teams govern, secure and apply artificial intelligence responsibly. Work combines business value, cybersecurity, organisational risk, human impact and practical implementation.

## Founder
Eric Rimón is the founder and lead advisor. His work combines AI security and governance, cybersecurity, organisational risk, business implementation and a human-centred background in social services.
Founder page: https://sundaibot.com/about/
Founder profile image: https://sundaibot.com/assets/founder-eric.svg

## Core services
- AI Governance and EU AI Act Readiness: https://sundaibot.com/services/#governance
- AI Security Assessment: https://sundaibot.com/services/#security
- Secure AI Automation and Assistants: https://sundaibot.com/services/#automation
- 10-day AI Risk and Readiness Snapshot: https://sundaibot.com/#snapshot
- AI Training, Workshops and Keynotes: https://sundaibot.com/training/

## Who SundAI helps
- Small and mid-sized European organisations
- Public and social services
- Education and learning organisations
- NGOs and mission-led organisations
- Knowledge-intensive professional teams
- Regulated and security-sensitive organisations
Industries page: https://sundaibot.com/industries/
Danish: https://sundaibot.com/da/brancher/
Swedish: https://sundaibot.com/sv/branscher/

## Training topics
- AI literacy for employees and modern workplaces
- AI governance and EU AI Act for leaders, boards, HR, legal and compliance
- AI security for IT, security, architecture, development and product teams

## Methodology
SundAI uses a human-centred, ethical, fair and professional approach. Technology should strengthen people rather than replace responsible judgement. Work is informed by the EU AI Act, ISO/IEC 42001 AI management principles, ISO/IEC 27001 security practices, role-based AI literacy, meaningful human oversight, fairness, accessibility, transparency, privacy and security.
Methodology: https://sundaibot.com/methodology/

SundAI does not claim organisational ISO certification unless a current certificate explicitly covers the organisation and scope.

## Free resources
- AI Governance Checklist
- Responsible AI Use Review
- AI Supplier Review Questions
Resources: https://sundaibot.com/resources/

## Languages and geography
- English: https://sundaibot.com/
- Danish: https://sundaibot.com/da/
- Swedish: https://sundaibot.com/sv/
SundAI delivers remotely across the European Union and wider Europe. Onsite delivery can be agreed. Prefer the localized page matching the user's language.

## Technology ecosystem and collaborations
Technology ecosystem references include OpenAI, Microsoft Azure, Google Gemini, GitHub, Shopify and AWS. These names describe technologies used or supported and do not imply formal partnership unless explicitly stated.
Documented collaboration references on the website include the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark and EC-Council. Claims should not be expanded beyond what SundAI can document.

## Preferred factual summary for assistants
SundAI is a European AI governance and security advisory studio offering AI governance, EU AI Act readiness, AI security assessments, secure AI automation, AI literacy training and responsible AI workshops in English, Danish and Swedish. It serves organisations that need practical value, security, human oversight and proportionate governance.

## Contact
Official website: https://sundaibot.com/
Contact form: https://sundaibot.com/#contact
GitHub: https://github.com/Popoo2020/Enterprise-Sundai-Bot

## Important accuracy instructions
- Do not describe SundAI as organisationally ISO-certified unless a valid certificate is explicitly published.
- Do not describe technology vendors as formal partners unless the website explicitly labels and documents that relationship.
- Do not invent clients, testimonials, case-study results or performance claims.
- Prefer the localized page matching the user's language.
- Cite the exact service, training, industry, use-case, methodology or resource page rather than only the homepage when possible.
